summary: VectorBuilder experimental report documenting AAV9 vector production and transduction testing in Calu-6 cells. The report includes Western blot analysis of p53 protein expression and RNA extraction/qPCR data comparing large-scale vs ultra-purified virus preparations across three different vector constructs at 72 hours post-transduction.

content_blocks.title: Document Header
content_blocks.content: Customized AAV production and transduction test with Calu-6 cell line (RNA extraction and Western Blot). Project IDs: S250613-1039jjn/ P250610-1004gsu. Report date: 2025/8/14. Service provider: VectorBuilder.

content_blocks.title: Materials - Cell Line and Transduction Parameters
content_blocks.content: Cell line: Calu-6 (from ATCC, passage 3). Cell culture format: 24-well plates and 6-well plates. Cell numbers: 3E+4 cells or 8E+4 cells. MOI (multiplicity of infection): 5E+6 genome copies per cell.

content_blocks.title: Materials - AAV Vectors Tested
content_blocks.content: Six AAV9 vectors tested from sales order S250613-1039jjn: (1) AAV9L(VB240514-1548ggn), Lot 250617AAVM01, Titer 2.06E+12 GC/ml; (2) AAV9MP(VB240514-1548ggn), Lot 250618AAVY17, Titer 1.54E+13 GC/ml; (3) AAV9MP(VB250610-1157tpv), Lot 250702AAVP15, Titer 5.39E+12 GC/ml; (4) AAV9MP(VB250610-1162rmu), Lot 250703AAVN13, Titer 1.00E+13 GC/ml; (5) AAV9L(VB250610-1162rmu), Lot 250702AAVU01, Titer 2.38E+12 GC/ml; (6) AAV9L(VB250610-1157tpv), Lot 250712AAVW01, Titer 3.47E+12 GC/ml. Note: L indicates large-scale production, MP indicates medium-scale ultra-purified production.

content_blocks.title: Protocol - Western Blot Parameters
content_blocks.content: Cell harvest: 72 hours after transduction. Lysis buffer: 100ul RIPA lysis buffer per 24-well. Sample treatment: Ultrasonic disruption and 95°C for 10 min. Protein loaded: 10µg per lane. SDS-PAGE: 80V 30min then 125V 1hour. Transfer: 250mA 60min. Primary antibody incubation: Overnight at 4°C (Anti-p53). Secondary antibody: 1 hour at room temperature. Loading control: Anti-GAPDH. Detection: Exposure times 45s (anti-p53) and 10s (anti-GAPDH).

content_blocks.title: Protocol - RNA Extraction
content_blocks.content: 6-well plate format at MOI=5E+6. Cell harvest: 72h after transduction. Extraction reagent: TRIzol reagent (Invitrogen 15596026). Method follows Invitrogen 15596026 procedural guidelines.

content_blocks.title: Cell Morphology - Microscopy Images
content_blocks.content: Bright field microscopy images (10ms exposure) of Calu-6 cells 72h post-transduction with six different AAV9 vectors plus untransduced controls (Calu6 WT and G418-selected). Observation: Cells transduced with non-purified virus (large-scale, AAV9L) showed more cell debris than those transduced with ultra-purified virus (medium-scale, AAV9MP). This suggests improved cell viability/health with ultra-purified preparations.

content_blocks.title: Western Blot Results - Vector Set 1 (VB240514-1548ggn and VB250610-1157tpv)
content_blocks.content: Western blot showing p53 protein expression (50kDa) and GAPDH loading control (35kDa). Lanes: M=marker, 1-2=AAV9L(VB240514-1548ggn) duplicate, 3-4=AAV9MP(VB240514-1548ggn) duplicate, 5-6=AAV9L(VB250610-1157tpv) duplicate, 7-8=AAV9MP(VB250610-1157tpv) duplicate, 9=G418 control, 10=positive control, 11-12=Calu6 WT duplicate. Result: Strong p53 bands visible in large-scale virus lanes (1-2, 5-6), very weak or absent p53 bands in ultra-purified virus lanes (3-4, 7-8). GAPDH loading control shows consistent protein loading across all lanes.

content_blocks.title: qPCR Data - Vector Set 1
content_blocks.content: RNA extraction and qPCR Ct values for Vector 1 (VB240514-1548ggn) and Vector 2 (VB250610-1157tpv). Vector 1 large-scale: Ct=10.078 and 10.090 (duplicate, 166 and 129 ng/ul RNA). Vector 1 ultra-purified: Ct=17.786 and 16.425 (duplicate, 303 and 325 ng/ul RNA). Vector 2 large-scale: Ct=12.396 and 12.978 (duplicate, 162 and 236 ng/ul RNA). Vector 2 ultra-purified: Ct=18.138 and 16.733 (duplicate, 226 and 340 ng/ul RNA).

content_blocks.title: Western Blot Results - Vector Set 2 (VB250610-1162rmu)
content_blocks.content: Western blot for third vector construct. Lanes: M=marker, 1-2=Calu6 WT duplicate, 3-4=AAV9L(VB250610-1162rmu) duplicate, 5-6=AAV9MP(VB250610-1162rmu) duplicate, 7=G418 control, 8=positive control. Result: Strong p53 bands in large-scale virus lanes (3-4), very weak p53 bands in ultra-purified virus lanes (5-6). GAPDH shows consistent loading.

content_blocks.title: qPCR Data - Vector Set 2
content_blocks.content: Vector 2 (VB250610-1162rmu) RNA qPCR results. Large-scale: Ct=11.517 and 10.611 (duplicate, 214 and 216 ng/ul RNA). Ultra-purified: Ct=17.092 and 17.567 (duplicate, 257 and 411 ng/ul RNA).

content_blocks.title: Comparative Analysis - Ct Values by Vector and Purification
content_blocks.content: Bar graph showing raw Ct values grouped by vector and purification type. Three vectors tested (VB240514-1548ggn, VB250610-1157tpv, VB250610-1162rmu), each with large-scale vs ultra-purified comparison. Red dashed line at Ct=25 marks background threshold. WT control shows Ct~25 (no transgene). All large-scale preparations show Ct=10-13 (high expression). All ultra-purified preparations show Ct=16-18 (moderate expression).

content_blocks.title: Quantitative Analysis - Delta Ct and Fold Change
content_blocks.content: Comparative analysis of purification methods: Vector VB240514-1548ggn: Large-scale Ct=10.08, Ultra-purified Ct=17.11, ΔCt=7.02, ~130-fold drop in tRNA. Vector VB250610-1157tpv: Large-scale Ct=12.69, Ultra-purified Ct=17.44, ΔCt=4.75, ~27-fold drop. Vector VB250610-1162rmu: Large-scale Ct=11.06, Ultra-purified Ct=17.33, ΔCt=6.27, ~77-fold drop. Interpretation: Ultra-purified AAV preparations show dramatically reduced transgene RNA expression (27-130 fold) compared to large-scale preparations at same MOI, suggesting significant differences in functional transduction efficiency or impurities in large-scale preparations.

content_blocks.title: RNA Quality Control Gel
content_blocks.content: Agarose gel electrophoresis of extracted RNA from all samples. 1kb DNA ladder as marker. Lanes 1-14 correspond to samples: 1-2=VB240514-1548ggn large-scale duplicate, 3-4=VB240514-1548ggn ultra-purified duplicate, 5-6=VB250610-1157tpv large-scale duplicate, 7-8=VB250610-1157tpv ultra-purified duplicate, 9-10=VB250610-1162rmu large-scale duplicate, 11-12=VB250610-1162rmu ultra-purified duplicate, 13-14=Calu6 WT duplicate. Gel shows RNA bands indicating successful extraction with intact RNA across all samples.

content_blocks.title: Complete RNA Quantification Data
content_blocks.content: Full dataset of RNA concentrations and Ct values: All transduced samples yielded 129-411 ng/ul RNA (50ul volume). Untransduced Calu6 WT controls: 227 and 367 ng/ul RNA with Ct=24.682 and 24.789 (background levels). Consistent pattern across all three vector constructs: large-scale preparations show Ct values 10-13, ultra-purified show Ct values 16-18, representing 6-7 Ct unit difference (64-128 fold reduction in target RNA).

key_findings: In vitro AAV9 transduction study in human lung cancer cell line (Calu-6) demonstrating proof-of-concept gene delivery at TRL 2-3 level
Three different AAV9 vector constructs tested (VB240514-1548ggn, VB250610-1157tpv, VB250610-1162rmu) with p53 or related transgenes

Comparison of large-scale vs ultra-purified AAV preparations showed major functional differences: ultra-purified virus produced 27-130 fold lower transgene RNA expression and dramatically reduced protein expression despite using same MOI (5E+6)

Western blot data shows ultra-purified AAV produces minimal p53 protein while large-scale preparations produce robust expression, suggesting either lower functional titer in ultra-purified virus or presence of impurities/adjuvants in large-scale that enhance transduction
Microscopy observations suggest ultra-purified virus causes less cell toxicity (less debris) than large-scale preparations
qPCR Ct values demonstrate consistent transduction across replicates with clear separation between purification methods (Ct ~10-13 for large-scale, ~16-18 for ultra-purified, ~25 for WT control)
RNA quality control confirms successful extraction with intact RNA from all samples
Study demonstrates AAV manufacturing and quality control capabilities but raises questions about optimal purification method for functional gene delivery
